---
title: Security Policy for {{REPOSITORY_NAME}}
description: Private vulnerability-reporting guidance and security support boundaries for {{REPOSITORY_NAME}}.
---

## Educational Repository Scope

{{REPOSITORY_NAME}} is an educational project, not a supported production
product. The maintainers accept responsible vulnerability reports, but do not
claim supported versions, guaranteed remediation, a response-time commitment,
or a bug bounty.

## Report A Vulnerability Privately

Email {{SECURITY_EMAIL}} with a clear subject that identifies the repository.
Include the affected path or component, impact, reproduction conditions, and
the minimum evidence needed to investigate safely.

Do not open a public Issue or Discussion for suspected vulnerabilities. Do not
send real credentials, personal data, confidential data, destructive payloads,
or unnecessary exploit material.

## Coordinated Disclosure

Allow maintainers a reasonable opportunity to investigate before public
disclosure. The maintainers may ask for clarification, coordinate a correction,
or determine that the report concerns sample code outside a supported use case.
Communication and remediation are best effort and are not an SLA.

## Outside Scope

* Production deployments or derivative systems not maintained by this repository
* Third-party services, extensions, packages, platforms, and their availability
* Exposed credentials or cloud resources created in a user's own account
* General support questions and non-security defects

Use {{SUPPORT_PATH}} for support routing and {{ISSUES_URL}} for ordinary defects.
